description: Upper GI bleed assessment and management
system_instruction: Focus on hemodynamic assessment, risk stratification, and management of upper gastrointestinal bleeding.

patient_persona.name: Robert Kowalski
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Black tarry stools and vomiting blood
patient_persona.background_story: History of NSAID use for chronic back pain. Noticed black stools 2 days ago, now vomiting bright red blood

initial_prompt: I've been passing black, sticky stools for two days. Today I started vomiting blood. I'm feeling really lightheaded and scared.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Acute Upper GI Bleed likely from Peptic Ulcer Disease

history_of_presenting_illness.onset: Black stools started 2 days ago, hematemesis started today
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Coffee-ground emesis now turning bright red, melena

medications: Ibuprofen 800mg TID for back pain (daily use past 3 months)

physical_exam_findings.abdomen: Soft, mild epigastric tenderness, no rebound, no guarding, no hepatosplenomegaly
physical_exam_findings.rectal: Melena (black, tarry stool) on digital exam
physical_exam_findings.skin: Pale, no jaundice, no spider angiomas

end_session.patient_response: I understand I need an endoscopy. Please help stop the bleeding.

critical_issue.response: Patient becoming hemodynamically unstable. Massive transfusion protocol and emergent endoscopy required.

evaluation_criteria.Hemodynamic_Assessment: Accurate assessment of hemodynamic status and resuscitation needs
evaluation_criteria.Risk_Stratification: Appropriate use of Glasgow-Blatchford or Rockall scores for risk stratification
evaluation_criteria.Resuscitation: Appropriate fluid resuscitation and blood product transfusion based on guidelines
evaluation_criteria.Pharmacotherapy: Appropriate use of proton pump inhibitors and octreotide
evaluation_criteria.Endoscopy_Timing: Appropriate determination of endoscopy timing and pre-endoscopy preparation
evaluation_criteria.NSAID_Cessation: Recognition and management of NSAID-induced ulcer risk

description: Evaluation and management of chronic diarrhea
system_instruction: Focus on diagnostic approach to chronic diarrhea, including stool studies, imaging, and treatment options.

case_metadata.title: Chronic Diarrhea - Inflammatory Bowel Disease Workup

patient_persona.name: Rachel Greenfield
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Chronic diarrhea with blood and abdominal pain for 3 months
patient_persona.background_story: Previously healthy, developed frequent loose stools with blood intermittently. Lost 8 lbs over 3 months. Symptoms affecting work and social life

initial_prompt: I've been having diarrhea for three months with blood in my stool sometimes. I have crampy abdominal pain and I'm running to the bathroom 6-8 times a day. I've lost weight and I'm always tired.

history_of_presenting_illness.character: Crampy abdominal pain, urgent loose stools with blood and mucus
history_of_presenting_illness.timing_and_duration: Daily for 3 months, intermittent symptoms initially now persistent

medications: Has tried loperamide OTC with minimal relief

social_history.diet_and_exercise: Regular diet, reduced exercise due to fatigue
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in apartment, works in marketing

physical_exam_findings.general: Thin female in no acute distress
physical_exam_findings.abdomen: Soft, mild left lower quadrant tenderness, no rebound, no masses, hyperactive bowel sounds
physical_exam_findings.rectal: Mild perianal skin irritation, digital exam reveals blood-streaked stool, no fissures or fistulas
physical_exam_findings.musculoskeletal: No joint swelling or tenderness despite history of arthralgias

colonoscopy_findings.extent: Proctosigmoiditis - inflammation extends to 35 cm from anal verge
colonoscopy_findings.findings: Continuous inflammation from rectum to sigmoid colon, erythema, friability, loss of vascular pattern, superficial ulcerations
colonoscopy_findings.histopathology: Active chronic colitis with crypt abscesses, architectural distortion, basal plasmacytosis consistent with ulcerative colitis

end_session.patient_response: Thank you for figuring out what's wrong with me. I'm ready to start treatment.

critical_issue.response: Patient reports significant increase in rectal bleeding. Requires urgent assessment and possible hospitalization.

evaluation_criteria.History_Taking: Comprehensive characterization of diarrhea including inflammatory vs non-inflammatory features
evaluation_criteria.Diagnostic_Workup: Appropriate use of stool studies, inflammatory markers, and endoscopic evaluation
evaluation_criteria.Differential_Diagnosis: Appropriate consideration of infectious, inflammatory, ischemic, and functional causes
evaluation_criteria.Colonoscopy_Interpretation: Accurate interpretation of endoscopic and histopathologic findings
evaluation_criteria.Treatment_Selection: Appropriate first-line therapy for moderate UC (5-ASA compounds)
evaluation_criteria.Patient_Education: Effective counseling on disease chronicity, treatment expectations, and when to seek urgent care
